soap_text: S: 68 y.o. M c/o severe LBP x 2 days after gardening. States difficulty urinating, feeling incomplete emptying. Reports new onset numbness in "private areas" and buttocks. Denies specific injury but states pain is 9/10. O: Gait antalgic. Diminished sensation noted on light touch in S2-S5 dermatomes bilaterally. LE strength unable to fully assess due to pain.

S: 32 y.o. F c/o worsening low back and leg pain for 1 week. Reports 2 episodes of accidental bowel leakage yesterday. States "legs feel heavy and weak" bilaterally, particularly when trying to stand from sitting. O: MMT reveals 3/5 strength in bilateral tibialis anterior and gastrocnemius. Perineal sensation decreased to pinprick.

description: 낙상 후 하지 무력 및 감각 이상
soap_text: S: 55 y.o. M presents after a fall down stairs. C/o severe LBP and inability to move his legs well. Reports "tingling and numbness around his anus" since the fall. Denies previous LBP. O: Bilateral LE active ROM limited due to weakness. MMT shows 2/5 in hip flexors and knee extensors bilaterally. Unable to stand without max assist.

S: 75 y.o. F with chronic LBP now worsened over 3 days. Reports new onset urinary incontinence, "can't feel when I need to go." Also notes a "dull ache" in her inner thighs and buttocks that feels different. O: Post-void residual scan pending. Sensation testing reveals decreased light touch in bilateral buttock and perineal region.

S: 42 y.o. M c/o severe LBP after lifting heavy boxes 2 days ago. Reports difficulty initiating urination and feeling like his bladder is always full. Notes weakness in both feet, making it hard to walk on heels. O: Bilateral ankle dorsiflexion MMT 3/5. Perineal sensation diminished to pinprick. Denies fever or chills.

S: 80 y.o. F c/o severe R hip pain after falling from standing height yesterday. O: Pt unable to bear weight on R LE due to 10/10 pain. Significant pain with any passive hip ROM. Suspicious for fracture.

S: 72 y.o. M presents with acute LBP 2 days post minor slip-and-fall onto buttocks. PMH significant for osteoporosis. O: Severe localized pain in lumbar spine, 8/10, with marked tenderness to percussion over L3-L4. Pain inhibits movement.

S: 91 y.o. F brought in after tripping over a rug at home. Complains of intense L groin pain, 9/10, unable to move L LE. O: LLE appears shortened and externally rotated. Any attempt at WB or movement causes excruciating pain.

S: 85 y.o. M reports sudden onset sharp mid-back pain after reaching overhead. PMH of long-term corticosteroid use. O: Localized T8 tenderness to palpation, exacerbated by deep breathing. Pain 7/10 at rest, 9/10 with movement. No recent trauma reported, but high index of suspicion.

S: 78 y.o. F c/o increasing R lateral hip pain over last week, following a minor sideways fall. PMH of osteoporosis for 10 yrs. O: Difficult to ambulate without severe pain (8/10) with each step. Tenderness to palpation over R greater trochanter.

S: 72 y.o. M c/o LBP x 3 mo. Constant dull ache, worse at night, wakes him from sleep. Denies trauma. Reports unintentional 10lb wt loss in last 2 mos.
O: ROM WNL but painful at end range. Palpation no specific tender points. SLR neg. Neuro screen intact. Pain non-mechanical pattern.

S: 58 y.o. F c/o R shoulder/neck pain x 4 wks. Hx of breast CA s/p mastectomy 5 yrs ago. Pain is constant ache, worse at rest & night. No relief with ice/heat.
O: Cervical ROM mildly restricted, painful. Shoulder AROM restricted in elevation/ER. MMT 4/5 R rotator cuff. Non-contractile pattern.

S: 44 y.o. M c/o deep R groin pain x 6 wks. Insidious onset. Worse at night, preventing sleep, and not relieved by rest or activity modification. Reports persistent fatigue.
O: Hip AROM limited in IR/flexion, painful. MMT strong. Pain non-capsular pattern. Lumbar screen neg.

S: 68 y.o. F c/o persistent midthoracic pain x 1 mo. Hx of lung CA s/p resection 2 yrs ago. Pain is constant, sharp, not affected by movement or position changes.
O: T-spine ROM WNL but painful. Palpation diffuse tenderness. Respiration unimpeded. Neuro screen intact. Pain non-mechanical.

description: 남성, 무릎 통증, 설명 불가 체중감소
soap_text:
S: 55 y.o. M c/o L knee pain x 2 mos. Denies injury. Pain deep ache, worsens significantly at night, keeps him awake. Reports feeling generally unwell & lost 15lbs w/o trying.
O: Knee ROM WNL, painful at end-range flexion/extension. Palpation diffuse tenderness. Effusion absent. MMT 5/5.

description: 요추수술 후 발열 및 안정시 통증
soap_text: S: 68 y.o. M c/o LBP x 2 wks, worsening since recent L4-L5 fusion 3 wks ago. Pain now constant, 8/10 at rest, wakes him at night. Reports feeling "off" and had a low-grade fever (100.2F) this morning. Denies recent falls or trauma.

S: 55 y.o. F with h/o poorly controlled DM and long-term steroid use presents with T-spine pain x 1 wk. Pain is deep, aching, 7/10, constant, and not relieved by position changes; worse at night. Reports generalized fatigue and intermittent subjective fever.

S: 32 y.o. M c/o severe neck pain radiating to R shoulder x 5 days. Pain described as throbbing, 9/10, much worse at night, preventing sleep. Pt reports h/o IV drug use and had a documented temperature of 101.5F yesterday.

S: 75 y.o. F presents with new onset sacral pain x 10 days, constant 6/10, not improving with rest or activity modification. Pain is deep and throbbing. Pt recently had hip replacement surgery 6 weeks ago. Reports feeling chills and had a temperature of 100.8F yesterday.

70 y/o M c/o LBP x 2 wks. States new onset of constant, deep abdominal pain, pulsating sensation noted sometimes. R LE feels colder than L LE, esp distally. Pain is constant, even at rest. Denies trauma.

65 y/o F c/o R LE pain x 3 days. R foot appears pale & feels cold to touch compared to L. Pain wakes her at night, unrelieved by position change. Reports increasing pain at rest. Diminished pedal pulses palpated.

58 y/o M c/o bilateral LE discomfort. States bilateral LE become pale and cool, esp. after walking short distances. Intermittent claudication now presenting as constant pain at rest in feet. R ankle-brachial index 0.7 (previous 0.9). Skin appears thin/shiny.

S: 72 y/o F c/o persistent LBP x 6 mos. Reports feeling "down" and "hopeless" most days, difficulty engaging in usual social activities due to pain & low energy. States pain is "always there" and rated 7/10 even at rest, despite stable objective findings last visit.

S: 45 y/o M with chronic neck pain. Reports averaging 4-5 hrs sleep/night due to pain, leading to significant fatigue and difficulty concentrating at work. States "always tired" & feels his pain is worse due to lack of rest.

S: 28 y/o F c/o severe R knee pain s/p twisting injury. Describes pain as "excruciating" 9/10, though able to ambulate w/ minimal limp. Reports pain flares dramatically with slightest movement, despite AROM/PROM within expected limits for this stage of recovery.

S: 55 y/o M s/p R rotator cuff repair, 4 wks post-op. Expresses fear of re-injury, actively guarding shoulder during AROM exercises. Reports "just can't do" prescribed home exercises, missing 2 out of 3 scheduled PT appts due to "feeling too sore."

S: 32 y/o F c/o chronic R wrist pain, desk job requiring extensive computer use. Reports high job demands, feeling overwhelmed by workload & tight deadlines, which she believes exacerbates her pain. States "can't afford to take time off" to rest.

S: 68 y/o M with generalized aches & pains. Expresses significant anxiety about future mobility and becoming a burden. Reports constantly checking symptoms and searching online, leading to increased worry and feeling "on edge."

S: 40 y/o F c/o persistent headaches x 3 mos. Reports feeling overwhelmed by family responsibilities and childcare, making it hard to manage her pain. States she often feels "on the verge of tears" and struggles to cope.

S: 22 y/o M s/p R ankle sprain 3 wks ago. Reports constant ache 6/10 despite minimal swelling & full ROM. Expresses significant fear of re-injury, avoiding full weight-bearing and reluctant to attempt single leg balance. Believes his ankle is "still too fragile."

S: 45 y.o. M c/o LBP s/p lifting heavy box 2 days ago. Pain 6/10, worse w/ fwd bending, better w/ rest. Denies night pain, bowel/bladder changes, fever, or unexplained wt loss. No hx of cancer. O: AROM WNL w/ mild pain end-range flexion.

S: 30 y.o. F c/o R shoulder pain x 3 wks, esp w/ overhead reaching. Pain 4/10, achy. No specific trauma, no fever or chills. Denies numbness/tingling into arm. O: AROM R shoulder flexion limited to 150 deg due to pain; empty can test mildly painful.

S: 25 y.o. M c/o ant L knee pain s/p inc running mileage 1 mo ago. Worse w/ stairs, prolonged sitting. No locking or giving way. Denies redness, warmth, recent falls, or fx hx. O: Patellar palpation mildly tender. No effusion noted.

S: 19 y.o. F c/o residual L ankle pain/stiffness 3 wks post inversion sprain. Pain 2/10, improving. Edema resolved. Full WB. No neuro sx. No new injury since initial sprain. O: Mild decrease in ankle dorsiflexion ROM compared to opp side.

S: 55 y.o. M c/o generalized neck stiffness and ache, worse in AM. No arm radicular sx. Denies dizziness, vision changes, or recent trauma. No constitutional sx. O: Cervical AROM full w/ minor end-range stiffness in rotation/side bending.

S: 38 y.o. F c/o diffuse LBP after prolonged desk sitting. Pain 3/10, relieved w/ standing/walking. No leg sx, no bowel/bladder changes. Denies constitutional sx, fever, or night pain. O: Lumbar posture shows mild increased kyphosis in sitting.

S: 60 y.o. M c/o R shoulder ache, esp at night when lying on it. Pain 5/10 w/ reaching behind back. No trauma, no fever or unexplained wt loss. Denies severe weakness or sudden ROM loss. O: Pain w/ resisted shoulder external rotation.

S: 70 y.o. F c/o L knee aching, worse w/ walking >30 min. Hx of OA. No new swelling or redness. No fever, no significant change in pain pattern. Denies DVT sx (redness, warmth, calf tenderness). O: Mild crepitus w/ knee flexion/extension.

S: 50 y.o. M c/o L heel pain, worse w/ 1st steps in AM and after rest. Pain 6/10. No hx of trauma. Denies redness, systemic sx, or nerve impingement sx. O: Pain w/ palpation of medial calcaneal tubercle. Limited ankle dorsiflexion.

S: 28 y.o. F c/o bilateral upper trap tension/soreness s/p increased computer work. Pain 4/10, dull ache. No headache, no visual changes. Denies paresthesias into arms or dizziness. O: Palpable tension and tenderness in bilateral upper trapezius muscles.
